title: cytisus hirsutus subsp. capitatus
intro: Cytisus hirsutus subsp. capitatus, also known as the hairy broom or Spanish broom, is a shrub-like legume native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used to treat various health issues. However, it is essential to note that Cytisus hirsutus subsp. capitatus contains toxic compounds called cytisine and N-oxides, which should be used with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
